title: Deployment Environments, OIDC, Workload Identity, and Cloud Authority · Release Evidence Brief: Ci Gcp Workload Identity Federation
summary: A deployment uses a long-lived service-account key in repository secrets, trusts any branch subject, grants project-wide admin, bypasses environment review, and leaves credentials reusable after the job. Planned artifact: Add and verify the ci gcp workload identity federation increment in environment and federated identity policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Exchange GitHub OIDC identity through a constrained Google Workload Identity Provider and service account without storing a key.
Request id-token write only in the deployment job and validate issuer, audience, subject, workflow ref, repository identity, event, and environment claims.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# cicd-github-actions cumulative delivery-system portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic workflow, policy, artifact, and release evidence review only.
# They never call GitHub APIs, start runners, execute learner commands, access Docker or registries, exchange OIDC tokens, or contact cloud services.

version-baseline:

  typescript-native: 7.0.2
  typescript-api-compatibility: 6.0.2
  build-push-action: 7
  google-auth: 3
  deploy-cloudrun: 3
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-delivery-environment

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example deployment stores a long-lived cloud key, requests broad id-token and repository permissions, and trusts ambiguous OIDC claims. Constrain environment, audience, subject, workflow, revision, service account, approval, and audit evidence. Competency probe: Exchange GitHub OIDC identity through a constrained Google Workload Identity Provider and service account without storing a key. A defensible solution applies Ci Gcp Workload Identity Federation, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to environment and federated identity policy.

content.code:
# Worked focus: ci-gcp-workload-identity-federation — Exchange GitHub OIDC identity through a constrained Google Workload Identity Provider and service account without storing a key.
  # Evidence: ci-worked-cd-environments-oidc-cloud-auth-4
  - competency: ci-worked-cd-environments-oidc-cloud-auth-4
    artifact: federated-deployment.yaml
    decision: worked cd environments oidc cloud auth 4
    environment: GitHub-hosted ubuntu-24.04 runner on a cold cache
    fault-injection: expire the only retained test artifact during incident reconstruction
    retained-proof: accessible redacted timeline with owner expiry and regression control
    case-key: worked4
    change: |
      environment: production
      reviewers: operations-and-security
      branch-policy: protected-main
      permissions: contents-read-id-token-write
      oidc-issuer: token.actions.githubusercontent.com
      oidc-audience: google-wif-provider
      subject: immutable-repository-workflow-environment
      google-auth-action: 3
      service-account: cloud-run-deployer
      roles: resource-scoped
      credential-lifetime: job-bounded
    verify: inspect environment history OIDC claims provider condition service-account impersonation IAM audit and credential cleanup
    failure: Reject long-lived keys, repository-name-only trust, global admin, approval-free environments, or short lifetime as sufficient control.
    repair: Constrain environment and claims, federate identity, separate service accounts, scope roles, audit exchange, expire credentials, and rehearse revocation.
content.caption: Runnable CI/CD workflow, artifact, release, and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.
